dtcs.description: SFR Solenoid Circuit

SM Solenoid Circuit These solenoids turn on when signals are received from the skid control ECU and they control the pressure acting on the wheel cylinders to control the braking force. NOTICE: When replacing the brake actuator assembly, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Calibration. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: RECONFIRM DTC HINT These codes are detected when a problem is determined in the brake actuator assembly. The solenoid circuits are in the brake actuator assembly. Therefore, solenoid circuit inspection and solenoid unit inspection cannot be performed. Be sure to check if any DTCs are output before replacing the brake actuator assembly. (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Start the engine. (c) Drive the vehicle at a speed of 20 km/h (12 mph) or more for 30 seconds or more. (d) Check if the same DTC is output    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result: HINT * If a speed signal of 6 km/h (4 mph) or more is input to the skid control ECU with the ignition switch ON and the stop light switch off, the ECU performs self diagnosis of the motor and solenoid circuits. * If the normal system code is output (no trouble codes are output), slightly jiggle the connectors, wire harness, and fuses of the brake actuator assembly. Make sure that no DTCs are output. * If any DTCs are output while jiggling a connector or wire harness of the brake actuator assembly (skid control ECU), inspect and repair the connector or wire harness. * It is suspected that the DTCs were output due to a bad connection of the connector terminal. B --  REPLACE BRAKE ACTUATOR ASSEMBLY  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Hydraulic Control Assembly - Antilock Brakes/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal A --  CHECK FOR INTERMITTENT PROBLEMS (SYMPTOM SIMULATION)  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting

dtcs.description: Open in ABS Motor Relay Circuit

Short in ABS Motor Fail Safe Relay Circuit The motor relay supplies power to the pump motor. While the brake control system is activated, the ECU turns the motor relay on and operates the pump motor. If the voltage supplied to the motor relay (+BM) is below the DTCs detection threshold due to low voltage from the battery or alternator, these DTCs may be stored. NOTICE: * Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. * When replacing the brake actuator assembly, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Calibration. HINT When C1241 and/or C1417 is output together with C0273, C0274 and/or C1361, inspect and repair the trouble areas indicated by

Low Power Supply Voltage Malfunction If a malfunction is detected in the power supply circuit, the skid control ECU (housed in the actuator assembly) stores this DTC and the fail-safe function prohibits ABS/TRAC/VSC operation. This DTC is stored when the IG1 terminal voltage deviates from the DTC detection condition due to a malfunction in the power supply or charging circuit such as the battery or alternator circuit, etc. The DTC is canceled when the IG1 terminal voltage returns to normal. NOTICE: * Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. * When replacing the brake actuator assembly, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Calibration. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Open in ABS Solenoid Relay Circuit

Short in ABS Solenoid Relay Circuit The solenoid relay supplies power to the ABS solenoids and TRAC solenoids. The solenoid relay is turned on 1.5 seconds after the ignition switch is turned to ON, and is turned off if an open or short in the solenoid is detected by self diagnosis performed when the vehicle starts running. The solenoid relay is housed in the skid control ECU in the actuator assembly. NOTICE: * Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. * When replacing the brake actuator assembly, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Calibration. HINT When C1241 and/or C1417 is output together with C0278 and/or C0279, inspect and repair the trouble areas indicated by C1241 and/or C1417 first See: C1241 or See: C1417). PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Yaw Rate Sensor (Test Mode DTC)

Acceleration Sensor Output Voltage Malfunction (Test Mode DTC) DTCs C0371 and C1279 can be cleared when the yaw rate and deceleration sensor sends a yaw rate and/or deceleration signal or when Test Mode ends. DTCs C0371 and C1279 are output only in Test Mode. NOTICE: When replacing the yaw rate sensor assembly, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Calibration. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Engine Control System Malfunction If a malfunction in the engine control system is detected, the operation of VSC and TRAC is prohibited by the fail-safe function. When the signals from the engine are input normally, the fail-safe control is canceled and the DTC is not stored. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Zero Point Calibration of Yaw Rate Sensor Undone

Zero Point Calibration of Acceleration Sensor Undone The skid control ECU receives signals from the yaw rate and deceleration sensor via the CAN communication system. The yaw rate sensor has a built-in deceleration sensor and detects the vehicle condition using 2 circuits (GL1, GL2). If there is trouble in the bus lines between the yaw rate and deceleration sensor and the CAN communication system, DTCs U0123 (malfunction in CAN communication with the yaw rate sensor) and U0124 (malfunction in CAN communication with the acceleration sensor) are output. The DTCs are also output when the calibration has not been completed. NOTICE: When replacing the brake actuator assembly or yaw rate sensor assembly, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Calibration. HINT When U0123, U0124 and/or U0126 is output together with C1210 and/or C1336, inspect and repair the trouble areas indicated by U0123, U0124 and/or U0126 first See: U Code Charts/U0073. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Steering Angle Sensor Zero Point Malfunction The skid control ECU acquires steering angle sensor zero point every time the ignition switch is turned to ON and the vehicle is driven at 35 km/h (22 mph) or more for approximately 5 seconds. The ECU also stores the previous zero point. If front wheel alignment or the steering wheel position is adjusted without disconnecting the negative battery terminal, or if yaw rate and deceleration sensor zero point is not acquired after the adjustments have been completed, the skid control ECU detects the difference between the previous zero point and newly acquired zero point and outputs this DTC to indicate a poor adjustment. NOTICE: When replacing the brake actuator assembly, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Calibration. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM ZERO POINT CALIBRATION OF YAW RATE AND DECELERATION SENSOR (a) Perform zero point calibration of the yaw rate and deceleration sensor    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Calibration. HINT * When the stored zero point of the yaw rate and deceleration sensor is cleared, steering angle sensor zero point will also be cleared. * If the zero point and output value of the yaw rate and deceleration sensor and the output value of the speed sensors are not normal, steering angle sensor zero point cannot be acquired normally even if the vehicle is driven straight ahead at 35 km/h (22 mph) or more. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK STEERING ANGLE SENSOR ZERO POINT CALIBRATION (a) Turn the ignition switch off. (b) Start the engine. (c) Drive the vehicle straight ahead at 35 km/h (22 mph) or more for at least 5 seconds. (d) Check that the center position of the steering wheel is correctly set while driving straight ahead. HINT If front wheel alignment and steering position are adjusted as a result of an abnormal center position of the steering wheel, acquire yaw rate and deceleration sensor zero point again after adjusting front wheel alignment and steering position again. OK: The center position of the steering wheel is correctly set. NG --  ADJUST FRONT WHEEL ALIGNMENT OR STEERING POSITION  See: Maintenance/Alignment/Service and Repair/Front Wheel Alignment OK -- Continue to next step.

(b) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (c) Start the engine. (d) Drive the vehicle at a speed of 35 km/h (22 mph) or more and turn the steering wheel to the right and left. (e) Check if the same DTC is output    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. dtcs.description: Acceleration Sensor Power Supply Voltage Malfunction The skid control ECU receives signals from the yaw rate and deceleration sensor via the CAN communication system. The yaw rate sensor has a built-in deceleration sensor and detects the vehicle condition using 2 circuits (GL1, GL2). If there is trouble in the bus lines between the yaw rate and deceleration sensor and the CAN communication system, DTCs U0123 (malfunction in CAN communication with the yaw rate sensor) and U0124 (malfunction in CAN communication with the acceleration sensor) are stored. NOTICE: When replacing the yaw rate sensor assembly, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Calibration. HINT When U0123, U0124 and/or U0126 is output together with C1381, inspect and repair the trouble areas indicated by U0123, U0124 and/or U0126 first See: U Code Charts/U0073. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: High Power Supply Voltage Malfunction If a malfunction is detected in the power supply circuit, the skid control ECU (housed in the brake actuator assembly) stores this DTC and the fail-safe function prohibits ABS/VSC/TRAC operation. This DTC is stored when the IG1 terminal voltage deviates from the DTC detection condition due to a malfunction in the power supply or charging circuit such as the battery or alternator circuit, etc. This DTC is canceled when the IG1 terminal voltage returns to normal. dtcs.description: Acceleration Sensor Internal Circuit

Yaw Rate Sensor Internal Circuit The skid control ECU receives signals from the yaw rate and deceleration sensor via the CAN communication system. The yaw rate sensor has a built-in deceleration sensor and detects the vehicle condition using 2 circuits (GL1, GL2). If there is trouble in the bus lines between the yaw rate and deceleration sensor and the CAN communication system, DTCs U0123 (malfunction in CAN communication with the yaw rate sensor) and U0124 (malfunction in CAN communication with the acceleration sensor) are stored. These DTCs are also output when calibration has not been completed. NOTICE: When replacing the yaw rate sensor assembly, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Calibration. HINT When U0123, U0124 and/or U0126 is output together with C1419 and/or C1435, inspect and repair the trouble areas indicated by U0123, U0124 and/or U0126 first See: U Code Charts/U0073. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: REPLACE YAW RATE SENSOR ASSEMBLY (a) Replace the yaw rate sensor assembly    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Acceleration/Deceleration Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal. NOTICE: Check the yaw rate and deceleration sensor signal after replacement    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Test Mode Procedure. HINT If troubleshooting has been carried out according to the Problem Symptoms Table, refer back to the table and proceed to the next step    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Symptom Related s/Vehicle Stability Control System/Problem Symptoms Table. (b) Reconnect the skid control ECU connector. (c) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (d) Start the engine. (f) Check if the same DTC is output    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result: HINT * If a speed signal of 6 km/h (4 mph) or more is input to the skid control ECU with the ignition switch ON and the stop light switch off, the ECU performs self diagnosis of the motor and solenoid circuits. * If the normal system code is output (no trouble codes are output), slightly jiggle the connectors, wire harness, and fuses of the brake actuator assembly. Make sure that no DTCs are output. * If any DTCs are output while jiggling a connector or wire harness of the brake actuator assembly (skid control ECU), inspect and repair the connector or wire harness. * It is suspected that the DTCs were output due to a bad connection of the connector terminal. dtcs.description: Steering Angle Sensor Power Source Voltage Malfunction Steering angle sensor signals are sent to the skid control ECU via the CAN communication system. When there is a malfunction in the CAN communication system, it is detected by the steering angle sensor zero point malfunction diagnostic function. HINT * When U0073, U0123, U0124 and/or U0126 is output together with C1432, inspect and repair the trouble areas indicated by U0073, U0123, U0124 and/or U0126 first See: U Code Charts/U0073. * When the speed sensor or the yaw rate and deceleration sensor has trouble, DTCs for the steering angle sensor may be output even when the steering angle sensor is normal. When DTCs for the speed sensor or yaw rate and deceleration sensor are output together with DTCs for the steering angle sensor, inspect and repair the speed sensor and yaw rate and deceleration sensor first, and then inspect and repair the steering angle sensor. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Yaw Rate Sensor Malfunction The skid control ECU receives signals from the yaw rate and deceleration sensor via the CAN communication system. The yaw rate sensor has a built-in deceleration sensor and detects the vehicle condition using 2 circuits (GL1, GL2). If there is trouble in the bus lines between the yaw rate and deceleration sensor and the CAN communication system, DTCs U0123 (malfunction in CAN communication with the yaw rate sensor) and U0124 (malfunction in CAN communication with the acceleration sensor) are stored. These DTCs are also output when calibration has not been completed. NOTICE: When replacing the yaw rate sensor assembly, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Calibration. dtcs.description: Steering Angle Sensor Initialization Incomplete

Vehicle Driven with Steering Angle Sensor not Initialized The skid control ECU acquires steering angle sensor zero point every time the ignition switch is turned to ON and the vehicle is driven at 40 km/h (25 mph) or more for approximately 10 seconds. The ECU also stores the previous zero point. The steering angle sensor zero point malfunction warning will be canceled by turning the ignition switch off. NOTICE: * Before proceeding with the inspection, explain to the customer that the output DTC is related to temporary loss of the steering angle zero calibration, such as when the power voltage drops when the battery is removed, and confirm that this condition occurred. * When U0073, U0123, U0124 and/or U0126 is output together with C1439 and/or C1445, inspect and repair the trouble areas indicated by U0073, U0123, U0124 and/or U0126 first See: U Code Charts/U0073. * When the speed sensor or the yaw rate and deceleration sensor has trouble, DTCs for the steering angle sensor may be output even when the steering angle sensor is normal. When DTCs for the speed sensor or yaw rate and deceleration sensor are output together with other DTCs for the steering angle sensor, inspect and repair the speed sensor and yaw rate and deceleration sensor first, and then inspect and repair the steering angle sensor See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Vehicle Stability Control System. * When the steering angle sensor and/or yaw rate sensor assembly is replaced, perform zero calibration for the applicable sensor(s) See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Test Mode Procedure. * When replacing the brake actuator assembly, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Calibration. PROCEDURE

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Reinstall motor rotation angle sensor connector. (b) Check for DTC. OK: DTC is not output. Result B --  END A -- Continue to next step.

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (MOTOR ROTATION ANGLE SENSOR) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the ignition switch ON. (c) Turn the Techstream on. (d) Check the motor rotation angle. dtcs.description: 25-C1534/25: ECU Malfunction

25 - EEPROM Malfunction If the power steering ECU detects these DTCs, it will shut off the motor relay circuit (built into the power steering ECU) and stop power assist. However, power assist continues if DTC C1533 is output. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: RECONFIRM DTC (a) Check for DTC. OK: DTC is not output. OK --  PROCEED TO NEXT CIRCUIT INSPECTION SHOWN IN PROBLEM SYMPTOMS TABLE NG --  REPLACE POWER STEERING ECU

dtcs.description: 25 - IG Power Supply Voltage Malfunction The power steering ECU distinguishes the ignition switch status as ON or off through the IG power source circuit. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (IG POWER SUPPLY) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the ignition switch ON. (c) Turn the Techstream on. (d) Check the IG power supply. Enter the following menus: Chassis / EMPS / Data List. EMPS OK --  CHECK INTERMITTENT PROBLEMS NG -- Continue to next step.
INSPECT FUSE (ECU-IG1) (a) Remove the ECU-IG1 fuse from the instrument panel junction block. (b) Measure the resistance of the fuse. OK: Below 1 Ohms NG --  REPLACE FUSE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (BATTERY - POWER STEERING ECU) (a) Disconnect the E18 and A1 power steering ECU connectors. (b) Measure the voltage of the wire harness side connectors. Standard voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE POWER STEERING ECU

dtcs.description: 22 - PIG Power Supply Voltage Malfunction When a problem occurs in the system, the power source relay circuit is shut off to stop the power assist. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (PIG POWER SUPPLY) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the ignition switch ON. (c) Turn the Techstream on. (d) Check the IG power supply. Enter the following menus: Chassis / EMPS / Data List. EMPS OK --  CHECK INTERMITTENT PROBLEMS NG -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (BATTERY - POWER STEERING ECU) (a) Disconnect the A1 power steering ECU connector. (b) Measure the voltage of the wire harness side connector. Standard voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE POWER STEERING ECU

dtcs.description: 23 - Power Supply Relay Failure If the power steering ECU detects these DTCs, it shuts off the motor relay circuit (built into the power steering ECU) and stops power assist. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: RECONFIRM DTC (a) Check for DTC. OK: DTC is not output. OK --  PROCEED TO NEXT CIRCUIT INSPECTION SHOWN IN PROBLEM SYMPTOMS TABLE NG --  REPLACE POWER STEERING ECU

dtcs.description: 26 - Assist Map Number Un-Writing The power steering ECU outputs this DTC when it determines that the assist map is not written in the ECU. HINT The assist map data is written in the power steering ECU to control assisting power. The assist map has 3 types. Select an assist map according to the vehicle country information that is recorded in the ECU. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM ASSIST MAP WRITING (a) Turn the ignition switch ON. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. HINT At this time, check that the P/S warning light is illuminated. (1) Select signal check, and change to test mode. (c) Wait for 3 seconds. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK P/S WARNING LIGHT (a) Check the P/S warning light. Result: HINT If the P/S warning light blinks at 0.25 second intervals, the assist map adjustment has completed normally. B --  END A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (a) Check for DTC. Result: HINT If DTC C1581/26 is output, turn the ignition switch off. Enter test mode again. If the DTC is output 3 times in a row, replace the power steering ECU. C --  PERFORM ASSIST MAP WRITING B --  REFER TO DTC A --  REPLACE POWER STEERING ECU

dtcs.description: 11-C2115/15: Transmitter ID1 Operation Stop

15 - Transmitter ID5 Operation Stop The tire pressure warning valve and transmitters that are installed in the tire and wheel assemblies measure the air pressure of the tires. The measured values are transmitted to the tire pressure warning antenna and receiver on the body as radio waves and then sent to the tire pressure warning ECU. The ECU compares the measured air pressure values with the air pressure threshold. When the measured air pressure value is less than this threshold, the NOTICE: It is necessary to register the transmitter IDs after replacing the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter and/or the tire pressure

steps.instruction: PERFORM FORCED TRANSMISSION OF TRANSMITTER ID OF ALL WHEELS (a) Set the tire pressure of all the wheels to the specified value    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Service Precautions. (b) Turn the ignition switch off. (c) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (d) Turn the ignition switch ON. (e) Turn the Techstream on. (f) Confirm that the data of tire pressure of all the tires (including the spare tire) are displayed on the Techstream display. Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List. Tire Pressure Monitor: HINT *1: It may take about 2 or 3 minutes until the values are displayed. If the values are not displayed after a few minutes, perform according to the inspection procedure for DTCs C2121/21 to C2125/25    See: C2121. (g) Rapidly reduce the tire pressure for each wheel at least 40 kPa (0.4 kgf/cm2, 5.8 psi) within 30 seconds. (1) Check that each tire pressure data displayed on the Techstream display has changed. OK: Each tire pressure data displayed on the Techstream display will change to the value of the tire pressure. NOTICE: * It takes about 2 or 3 minutes to display the updated tire pressure data. * If "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data has not changed, reset the tire pressure to the appropriate specified value and rotate the tire 90 to 270 degrees (including the spare tire). Then rapidly reduce the tire pressure and recheck it. (2) After confirming that all of the tire pressure data displayed on the Techstream display has changed, set the tire pressure to the appropriate specified values. HINT If the tire pressure data displayed on the Techstream display has not changed after rechecking, inspect for another problem    See: C2121. NG --  GO TO TRANSMITTER AND RECEIVER INSPECTION PROCEDURE  See: C2121 OK --  END

dtcs.description: 21-C2125/25,C2181/81-C2185/85: No Signal from Transmitter ID1

The measured values are transmitted to the tire pressure warning antenna and receiver on the body as radio waves and then sent to the tire pressure warning ECU. The ECU compares the measured air pressure values with the air pressure threshold. When the measured air pressure value is less than this threshold, the NOTICE: It is necessary to register the transmitter IDs after replacing the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter and/or the tire pressure

dtcs.description: 73 - Vehicle Speed or Engine Speed Signal Malfunction The tire pressure warning ECU receives a vehicle speed signal from the combination meter and an engine speed signal from the ECM. The tire pressure NOTICE: It is necessary to register the transmitter IDs after replacing a tire pressure warning valve and transmitter and/or the tire pressure

(b) Drive the vehicle at 20 km/h (12 mph) or more for 10 seconds to erase DTCs C2191/91 and C2194/94. (c) Check the test mode DTCs. CHECK FREQUENCY RECEIVING CONDITION (a) Check that the vehicle is not located in an area described below: (1) Facilities or devices that use similar radio frequencies are located in the vicinity of the vehicle. HINT If the vehicle is located in an area described above, the tire pressure warning light may come on only in a particular area. (2) Devices using similar radio frequencies are used in the vehicle. OK: Facilities, or devices that use similar radio frequencies are not located in the vicinity of the vehicle. HINT Radio frequencies may be interrupted due to surroundings or devices installed by the user. NG --  CHECK IF ANY DEVICE IS INSTALLED BY USER OK -- Continue to next step.

IDENTIFY TRANSMITTER CORRESPONDING TO DTC (a) Set the tire pressure of all the wheels to the specified value    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Service Precautions. (b) Turn the ignition switch off. (c) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (d) Turn the ignition switch ON. (e) Turn the Techstream on. (f) Check the values by referring to the table below. Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List. Tire Pressure Monitor: HINT *1: It may take about 2 or 3 minutes until the values are displayed. NOTICE: * It takes about 2 or 3 minutes to display the updated tire pressure data. * If "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data has not changed, reset the tire pressure to the appropriate specified value and rotate the tire 90 to 270 degrees. Then rapidly reduce the tire pressure and recheck it. * Record the transmitter ID of which "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data corresponds to each tire. (i) After confirming that one of "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data for one tire (ID1 to ID5) has changed, repeat this procedure one by one. Identify the transmitter that corresponds to the DTC. Result: C --  INSPECT TIRE PRESSURE WARNING VALVE AND TRANSMITTER B --  END A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (ECU - RECEIVER) (a) Disconnect the E56 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the L26 receiver connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

(e) Check the values by referring to the table below. Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List. Tire Pressure Monitor: HINT *1: Displayed only when the ID No. is not registered. (f) Check the ID number on the identified transmitter by removing it from the tire and wheel. (g) Confirm that the ID number on the transmitter and recorded transmitter ID match. Result: B --  REGISTRATION OF TRANSMITTER ID A -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING VALVE AND TRANSMITTER (a) Replace the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

REGISTRATION OF TRANSMITTER ID (a) Perform the registration    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

(e) Check the values by referring to the table below. Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List. Tire Pressure Monitor: HINT * *1: It may take about 2 or 3 minutes until the values are displayed * If "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data has not changed, reset the tire pressure to the appropriate specified value and rotate the tire 90 to 270 degrees. dtcs.description: 26 - Transmitter ID not Received in Main Mode After all IDs are registered, DTC C2126/26 is set in the tire pressure warning ECU and the tire pressure warning light blinks for 1 minute and then comes on. When the tire pressure warning ECU successfully receives radio waves from all the transmitters whose IDs are stored in the ECU, DTC C2126/26 is deleted and the tire pressure warning light goes off. HINT If the IDs stored in the tire pressure warning ECU differ from the transmitter IDs, DTC C2126/26 is set. Check that the IDs in the ECU are the same as the transmitter IDs. NOTICE: It is necessary to register the transmitter IDs after replacing a tire pressure warning valve and transmitter and/or the tire pressure

(i) After confirming that the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data for one tire (ID1 to ID5) has changed, repeat this procedure one by one. Identify the transmitter not received. dtcs.description: 41-C2145/45: Transmitter ID1 Error

45 - Transmitter ID5 Error The tire pressure warning valve and transmitters that are installed in the tire and wheel assemblies measure the air pressure of the tires. The measured values are transmitted to the tire pressure warning antenna and receiver on the body as radio waves and then sent to the tire pressure warning ECU. The ECU compares the measured air pressure values with the air pressure threshold. When the measured air pressure value is less than this threshold, the NOTICE: It is necessary to register the transmitter IDs after replacing the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter and/or the tire pressure

steps.instruction: IDENTIFY TRANSMITTER CORRESPONDING TO DTC (a) Set the tire pressure of all the wheels to the specified value    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Service Precautions. (b) Turn the ignition switch off. (c) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (d) Turn the ignition switch ON. (e) Turn the Techstream on. (f) Rapidly reduce the tire pressure for each wheel at least 40 kPa (0.4 kg/cm2, 5.8 psi) within 30 seconds. If "ID Inflation Pressure" displayed on the tester (ID1 to ID5) does not change, the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter corresponding to the unchanged "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data was the cause of the output DTC. HINT * Identify the malfunctioning tire pressure warning valve and transmitter by repeatedly decreasing the tire pressure for each tire. * Record which "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data (ID1 to ID5) corresponds to which tire. Tire Pressure Monitor: HINT *1: It may take about 2 or 3 minutes until the values are displayed. If the values are not displayed after a few minutes, perform according to the inspection procedure for DTCs C2121/21 to C2125/25    See: C2121. (g) Check the Data List. Result: NOTICE: * If "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data has not changed, reset the tire pressure to the appropriate specified value and rotate the tire 90 to 270 degrees. Then forcibly transmit the transmitter ID and recheck it. * Record the transmitter ID and position of transmitters that are normal. (h) When the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data (ID1 to ID5) has changed, repeat this procedure to identify the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter that corresponds to the DTC. (i) When all of the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data (ID1 to ID5) have changed, identify the malfunctioning tire pressure warning valve and transmitter using recorded ID numbers and output DTC. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING VALVE AND TRANSMITTER (a) Replace the identified tire pressure warning valve and transmitter with a new one    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal. HINT * Before installing a new tire pressure warning valve and transmitter, read and write down its transmitter ID. * The IDs for the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter not to be replaced should be checked using the Techstream and recorded. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

REGISTRATION OF TRANSMITTER ID (a) Register the transmitter ID for all the wheels    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (TIRE INFLATION PRESSURE) (a) Turn the ignition switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the ignition switch ON. (d) Turn the Techstream on. (e) Check the values by referring to the table below. Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List. Tire Pressure Monitor: HINT * *1: It may take about 2 or 3 minutes until the values are displayed. * When no "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data has changed, reset the tire pressure to the appropriate specified valve and rotate the tire 90 to 270 degrees. Then rapidly release the tire pressure and recheck it. Result: B --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal A --  END

dtcs.description: 65-C2169/69: Abnormal Temperature Inside ID1 Tire

69 - Abnormal Temperature Inside ID5 Tire Each tire pressure warning valve and transmitter measures the internal temperature of its tire as well as tire pressure, and transmits the information to the tire pressure warning ECU along with the transmitter ID. If the measured temperature is out of the specified range, the tire pressure warning ECU recognizes it as a malfunction, outputs DTCs, and the tire pressure warning light blinks for 1 minute and then remains on. HINT It is necessary to perform the procedure to identify the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter that is malfunctioning because it cannot be identified by the output DTC. NOTICE: It is necessary to register the transmitter IDs after replacing the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter and/or the tire pressure

steps.instruction: CHECK TIRES (a) Check the tires are not flat and there are no indication of air pressure drop. OK: Tires are normal. Then forcibly transmit the transmitter ID and recheck it. * Record the transmitter ID and position that is normal condition. dtcs.description: 76 - Receiver Error When the tire pressure warning ECU is detected to internal data error, DTC C2176/76 is stored. NOTICE: It is necessary to register the transmitter IDs after replacing the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter and/or the tire pressure

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC OUTPUT (C2176/76) (a) Clear the DTC    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the ignition switch off. (c) Turn the ignition switch ON and check for DTCs    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK:

dtcs.description: 91 - Vehicle Speed Signal Error (Test Mode DTC) The tire pressure warning ECU receives a vehicle speed signal from the combination meter. This DTC is stored upon entering test mode, and cleared when a vehicle speed signal of 20 km/h (12 mph) is detected for 3 seconds or more. This DTC is output only in test mode. NOTICE: It is necessary to register the transmitter IDs after replacing the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter and/or the tire pressure

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (VEHICLE SPEED) (a) Turn the ignition switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the ignition switch ON. (d) Turn the Techstream on. (e) Check that the values indicated on the Techstream and on the combination meter are the same. Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List. Tire Pressure Monitor: OK: Vehicle speed indicated on the Techstream and combination meter are the same. NG --  INSPECT TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU OK --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting

INSPECT TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU (a) Disconnect the E56 ECU connector. (b) Jack up the vehicle. (c) Shift the lever to the neutral position. (d) Turn the ignition switch ON. (e) Check the waveform of the ECU connector using an oscilloscope, while turning the wheel slowly. OK: Tool Setting HINT The wavelength becomes shorter as the vehicle speed increases. NG --  CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (ECU - COMBINATION METER) OK --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (ECU - COMBINATION METER) (a) Disconnect the E56 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the E19 meter connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE COMBINATION METER  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 94 - Engine Speed Signal Error (Test Mode DTC) The tire pressure warning ECU receives an engine speed signal from the ECM. This DTC is stored upon entering test mode, and cleared when the engine speed signal of 1000 rpm is detected for 3 seconds or more. This DTC is output only in test mode. NOTICE: It is necessary to register the transmitter IDs after replacing a tire pressure warning valve and transmitter and/or the tire pressure

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (ENGINE SPEED) (a) Turn the ignition switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the ignition switch ON. (d) Turn the Techstream on. (e) Check that the values indicated on the Techstream and on the combination meter are the same. Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List. Tire Pressure Monitor: OK: Engine speed indicated on the Techstream and on the combination meter are the same. NG --  INSPECT TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU (TACH SIGNAL) OK --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting

INSPECT TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU (TACH SIGNAL) (a) Disconnect the E56 ECU connector. (b) Start the engine. (c) Check the waveform of the ECU connector using an oscilloscope, while idling the engine. OK: Tool Setting HINT The wavelength becomes shorter as the engine speed increases. NG --  CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU - ECM) OK --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU - ECM) (a) Disconnect the E56 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the A9 ECM connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. (1) for 2GR-FE: Standard resistance: (2) for 2AR-FE: Standard resistance: Result: C --  REPLACE ECM (for 2GR-FE)  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal B --  REPLACE ECM (for 2AR-FE)  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal A --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR
